question.text: SEBI has renamed the "dividend option" in mutual fund schemes as:

answer.text: Income Distribution cum Capital Withdrawal (IDCW) option

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** SEBI renamed the dividend options to clearly communicate to investors that under the so-called "dividend" option, a portion of their capital can also be distributed as dividend. The new name "Income Distribution cum Capital Withdrawal (IDCW)" more accurately reflects that payouts may include return of capital, not just income earned. There are two sub-options: pay-out of IDCW and re-investment of IDCW.
**The Trap:** The other options are fabricated terms. The key insight is that MF "dividends" are different from company dividends — they can include a return of the investor's own capital, which is why SEBI mandated the name change for transparency.
**Cross-Exam Context:** MF topics overlap with Series V-A, V-B. This renaming is a recent regulatory development and is commonly tested in updated exam syllabi.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Many investors still say "dividend option" — gently educate them that it is now IDCW. More importantly, explain that IDCW payouts reduce the NAV proportionally and may include their own capital being returned, not just profits.
